records.ety: [Gr. <?/, fr. <?/ to show or shine through; dia` through + <?/ to show, and in the passive, to shine: cf. F. diaphane. See Phantom, and cf. Diaphane, Diaphanic.]

defs: Allowing light to pass through, as porcelain; translucent or transparent; pellucid; clear.
quotes: Another cloud in the region of them, light enough to be fantastic and diaphanous.
